member.name: Blair McDougall
member.synopsis: Blair McDougall is the Labour MP for East Renfrewshire , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024. He currently holds the Government post of Parliamentary Under Secretary of State (Department for Business and Trade).

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Universal Credit: Disability
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the availability of residential training places for disabled young people in Scotland on proposed changes to eligibility for Universal Credit for
writtenQuestions.answerText: No assessment has been made. We welcomed views on raising the age someone can access the Universal Credit Health Element to 22, as part of our Green Paper consultation, which closed on 30 June. We are considering the responses to this consultation, and we...

writtenQuestions.heading: X Corp: Artificial Intelligence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, if he will hold discussions with X.com on the potential impact of the output of its Grok AI on community safety.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government’s commitment to tackling online harms applies across all social media companies, and we engage with all major platforms in this regard. Many generative AI services have functionalities that bring them into scope of the Online Safety Act – s...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Departments: X Corp
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Minister for the Cabinet Office, whether the Government plans to continue to use X.com as a platform for government communications.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government uses a range of channels to reach and engage the public. Use of these platforms is assessed against the high standards for digital safety, as set out in the Government Communication Service (GCS) SAFE framework.

writtenQuestions.heading: St Lucia: Crimes of Violence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, how many consular cases involving violent crime his Department has dealt with in St Lucia in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Between 2019 and 2024 British nationals sought consular assistance as a result of violent crime on 15 occasions.

writtenQuestions.heading: British Nationals Abroad: Crimes of Violence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, how many consular cases involving violent crime against British tourists his Department has dealt with for customers of Tui Group.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Whether or not a British national was travelling with a particular tour company, airline or other business when they sought consular assistance is not captured in our case management system.

writtenQuestions.answerText: The British National (Overseas) (BN(O)) route was launched on 31 January 2021 in response to China’s passing of the National Security Law. The route reflects the UK’s historic and moral commitment to those people of Hong Kong who chose to retain their tie...

writtenQuestions.answerText: Border Force does not routinely assess whether goods on freight entering the UK may have been made using forced labour, but we work closely with law enforcement partners to share intelligence to ensure all goods abide by customs and excise rules.The Gover...

writtenQuestions.heading: Government Departments: Procurement

writtenQuestions.answerText: The UK Government is committed to tackling human rights abuses including modern slavery and human trafficking in public supply chains. On 24 February 2025 the Procurement Act came into force, providing contracting authorities with stronger powers to exclu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Antisemitism
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for the Home Department, what steps she is taking to protect Jewish communities from terrorist attacks.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In her speech to the annual dinner of the Community Security Trust on 26 March, the Home Secretary made clear the Government’s commitment to protect Jewish communities against antisemitic attacks and other security threats. The full speech is available he...

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy Drinks: Children
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, whether his Department plans to review the regulations on the sale of energy drinks to children under the age of 16.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the King’s Speech, we plan to bring forward the necessary secondary legislation to deliver on our commitment to ban the sale of high-caffeine energy drinks to children under the age of 16 years old.We are developing plans which consider the ...
